smil_patterns: any SMIL entrance effect mapped to a PPT animEffect filter
entr/filter_effect.source: generalisation of .visual_tmp/samples/experiment 7.pptx entrance presets — single template parameterised by FILTER string
entr/filter_effect.notes: Universal entrance effect template. Parameterised by FILTER (one of the PowerPoint-recognised filter strings: fade, dissolve, wipe(down|up|left|right), wedge, wheel(1|2|3|4|8), circle(in|out), strips(downLeft|downRight|upLeft|upRight), blinds(horizontal|vertical), checkerboard(across|down), barn(inVertical|inHorizontal|outVertical|outHorizontal), randombar(horizontal|vertical)) plus PRESET_ID/PRESET_SUBTYPE for Animation Pane round-trip fidelity. Structure identical to entr/fade — the only dynamic piece is the <p:animEffect filter="{FILTER}"> attribute. The <p:set> style.visibility→visible primer fires for 1ms at the start so PPT's build engine knows to reveal the pre-hidden shape. Requires the multi-slide harness (blank slide 1 → animated slide 2) so PPT pre-hides before the click-group triggers. Verified via .visual_tmp/filter_sweep.py: all 16 tested filter values (fade, dissolve, wipe(down|up|left|right), wedge, wheel(1|2), circle(in|out), strips(downLeft), blinds(horizontal), checkerboard(across), barn(inVertical), randombar(horizontal)) animate correctly. wipe(down) shows top-down directional reveal, circle(in) shows elliptical reveal, checkerboard(across) shows checker pattern reveal — each filter renders its signature animation through this single template.

smil_patterns: any SMIL exit effect mapped to a PPT animEffect filter
exit/filter_effect.source: generalisation of .visual_tmp/samples/experiment 7.pptx exit presets — single template parameterised by FILTER string
exit/filter_effect.notes: Universal exit effect template. Same filter vocabulary as entr/filter_effect but with transition="out". Child order is animEffect FIRST then set (opposite of entrance), with the set firing at SET_DELAY_MS (typically duration-1) so the shape stays visible for the full animation and only disappears at the last moment. PRESET_ID/PRESET_SUBTYPE carry the exit preset identity for Animation Pane round-trip fidelity. Exit filters share the entrance filter vocabulary — the same fade/dissolve/wipe/wedge/wheel/circle/strips/blinds/checkerboard/barn/randombar values work with transition="out". Structural equivalence to entr/filter_effect (same child primitives in mirrored order) carries the verification from the entrance sweep; individual exit filter values were not swept but the existing exit/fade slot which uses an identical shape is separately visually-verified.

entr/fade.notes: Exact structural match to starter-deck slide7 preset 9 entrance fade. Verified in Microsoft PowerPoint for Mac via tools/visual/animation_tune fade_in: slide-area pixel mean transitions from white (pre-hide) through a faint-blue mid-fade frame to full shape colour. Requires the multi-slide harness so the build engine pre-hides before the click-group fires.

exit/fade.notes: Child order is animEffect then set (opposite of entr/fade). The visibility set fires at SET_DELAY_MS (typically duration_ms - 1) so the shape stays visible until the fade-out completes. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune exit_fade: frame 1 shows shape visible on slide 2 pre-trigger, frame 2+ shows shape gone after the fade-out. Preset ID 9 is carried over from entr/fade; the oracle corpus has no preset 9 exit fade sample, only 31/37/47/53 with richer children (motion + fade + set). Revisit preset ID when a matching sample is captured.

entr/appear.notes: Outer cTn carries NO dur attribute — fill='hold' alone makes the visibility set persist. Adding dur (or wrapping in build_delayed_child_par which supplies its own dur) causes PowerPoint to expire the container and revert the set, making the shape disappear after the animation completes. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune appear_visible: frame 0 blank slide 1, frame 1 transitions to slide 2 with the set already fired, frames 2+ hold at the visible state.

emph/color.notes: TARGET_ATTRIBUTE = fill.color|line.color|shadow.color etc. INNER_FILL is the SVG fill_mode mapped to PPT 'hold' or 'remove'. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune color_change: slide-area mean transitions from red-tinted (235, 221, 228) at frame 1 to blue (218, 222, 247) at frames 5+. CAVEAT: oracle corpus has an animClr example with presetID=27 nodeType=clickEffect grpId=0 using schemeClr (different preset, cleaner inner cTn) — our preset 7 withEffect shape plays correctly but is not the exact shape PPT authors for 'Change Fill Color'. Revisit when an oracle sample for preset 7 emph is captured.

emph/rotate.notes: ROTATION_BY is in 60000ths of a degree (PPT angle factor). INNER_FILL is the SVG fill_mode mapped to 'hold' or 'remove'. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune rotate_spin (90-degree quarter turn): shape bbox visibly changes orientation between the pre-rotation and post-rotation frames.

emph/scale.notes: Scale values in 100000ths (PPT scale factor). INNER_FILL is the SVG fill_mode mapped to 'hold' or 'remove'. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune scale_grow: non-background pixel share climbs from 41 (baseline) through 45 (mid-grow) to 54 (settled) as the shape doubles in size. Companion animMotion for origin compensation is NOT emitted by this template alone — see emph/scale_with_origin for the composed version when authored.

smil_patterns: any stacked combination of animate/animateColor/animateMotion/animateTransform/set on one element
emph/compound.source: empirically verified via .visual_tmp/xmas_tree_v2.py — 11 heterogeneous child behaviors under ONE cTn fire simultaneously on a single click
emph/compound.notes: Universal compound slot. The template is a bare <p:par>/<p:cTn>/<p:childTnLst> scaffold with no preset ID and an empty childTnLst — callers use AnimationOracle.instantiate_compound(behaviors=[...]) instead of instantiate(...) to inject behavior fragment children from src/svg2ooxml/assets/animation_oracle/emph/behaviors/*.xml. Each fragment is one or more raw <p:set>/<p:animEffect>/<p:animClr>/<p:animRot>/<p:animScale>/<p:animMotion>/<p:anim> elements that are substituted with their own tokens and appended as direct children of this cTn. PPT's rendering engine does NOT require preset IDs to fire the child behaviors — presetID/presetSubtype are cosmetic UI metadata only. This slot subsumes every single-preset slot because any combination of behaviors can be composed inside one cTn. Build mode is 'paragraph' (build="p" rev="1") so text-paragraph fragments (bold, underline, text_color, text_scale) fire correctly alongside shape fragments. Verified end-to-end via the xmas tree v2 experiment: 8 effects (transparency + shape_fill_color + text_color + bold + underline + rotate + scale + motion) stacked in one cTn, all played simultaneously on one Next keypress with delta_max 7.51 (same as the nested-par v1 with 27 XML elements vs this v2's 11).

emph/transparency.notes: PPT's 'Transparency' emphasis — fades the WHOLE SHAPE (fill + stroke + text together) to a partial opacity and holds there. Uses the legacy <p:animEffect filter="image" prLst="opacity: X"> with a paired <p:set> on style.opacity. TARGET_OPACITY is a decimal string like '0.3' (NOT a PPT fltVal). The outer <p:iterate type="lt"><p:tmAbs val="0"/></p:iterate> is boilerplate PPT emits but does not restrict the effect to text. Verified visually via .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py: frame 0 shows opaque rectangle, frame 5 shows ~30% transparent rectangle with faded text.

emph/text_color.notes: PPT's 'Change Font Color' emphasis. Distinct from emph/color which targets shape fill via 'fillcolor' attrName — this targets 'style.color' (the text color inside the shape). Uses per-letter iterate (tmPct=0, simultaneous) and <p:cBhvr override="childStyle">. TO_COLOR is the 6-hex srgbClr value (e.g. 'E81E1E'). Verified visually via .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py: frame 5 shows text tinted to target color while rectangle fill unchanged.

emph/stroke_color.notes: PPT's 'Change Line Color' emphasis. Pairs <p:animClr> on 'stroke.color' with a companion <p:set> on 'stroke.on'→'true' as a primer to ensure the stroke is visible (mirrors the fillcolor + fill.on + fill.type triple pattern). No iterate — per-shape effect, not per-letter. TO_COLOR is the 6-hex srgbClr value. Verified visually via .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py: frame 15 shows a clear red border around the rectangle that was borderless at frame 0.

emph/bold.notes: PPT's 'Bold' emphasis. Applies <p:set> on 'style.fontWeight' to 'bold' via childStyle override, targeting <p:txEl><p:pRg st="0" end="0"/></p:txEl> for the first paragraph. Uses per-letter iterate with tmAbs=25 (25ms stagger between letters) for a typewriter-like cascade. REQUIRES bldP with build="p" rev="1" — build_timing_tree must emit paragraph-mode bldP entries for the animated group. Verified visually: text goes from non-bold to bold letter-by-letter.

emph/underline.notes: PPT's 'Underline' emphasis. <p:set> on 'style.textDecorationUnderline' to 'true' via childStyle override, targeting first paragraph. Uses tmPct=4000 (40% stagger) so each letter's underline appears with a staggered cascade. REQUIRES bldP with build="p" rev="1". Verified visually: underline appears under each letter sequentially.

emph/shape_fill_color.notes: PPT's 'Change Fill Color' emphasis scoped to the shape background (NOT text). This is the native PPT path for animating the shape's fill color — distinct from the legacy emph/color slot which targets fillcolor without <p:bg/>. Uses the quadruple primer pattern: <p:animClr> style.color + <p:animClr> fillcolor + <p:set> fill.type→solid + <p:set> fill.on→true, all targeting <p:spTgt spid=X><p:bg/></p:spTgt>. The quadruple ensures the fill is visible (fill.on=true), solid (fill.type=solid), and tweened on both style.color and fillcolor simultaneously (schemeClr-compatible dual path). TO_COLOR is a 6-hex srgbClr value. Verified visually via .visual_tmp/transparency_experiment.py: rectangle fill tweens blue → red at frame 15, text stays white (not part of target). In example5.pptx/experiment 6.pptx this preset also has a withEffect sibling with same grpId targeting <p:txEl><p:pRg> for combined fill+text color change — that full compound should use this slot PLUS emph/text_color in parallel.

emph/color_pulse.notes: PPT's 'Color Pulse' emphasis on text. Same quadruple structure as emph/shape_fill_color but with: (1) <p:txEl><p:pRg st="0" end="0"/> target for text instead of <p:bg/>, (2) autoRev="1" fill="remove" on inner cTns so the color flashes forward then reverses to original, (3) outer cTn fill="remove", (4) <p:iterate type="lt"><p:tmPct val="0"/></p:iterate> for per-letter simultaneous firing. Duration governs the single-direction flash (total effect = 2×DURATION_MS due to autoRev). Verified visually: text Experiment flashes to red at frame 5, returns to white after capture window. Requires bldP build="p" rev="1" for paragraph-level firing.

emph/blink.notes: PPT's 'Blink' emphasis. <p:anim calcmode="discrete"> with two tav keyframes on style.visibility: hidden at tm=0, visible at tm=50000 (midpoint). No iterate — whole-shape effect. For repeat/multi-blink patterns the handler should add repeatCount to the outer cTn. Verified visually: rectangle disappears for half the duration then reappears.

path/motion.notes: PATH_DATA is a PPT motion-path string (coordinates normalized to 0-1, ends with E). NODE_TYPE is 'clickEffect' for standalone motion (motion.py) or 'withEffect' for composed motion (numeric.py width→scale+motion). INNER_FILL is the SVG fill_mode mapped to 'hold' or 'remove'. Verified via tools/visual/animation_tune motion_translate: shape centre measured at (1141, 803) pre-trigger and (1457, 882) post-motion — ~1 inch right, ~0.3 inch down, matching the authored path. Does NOT express rotation (rAng) — motion animations with auto-rotate or fixed-rAng fall through to the imperative path.
